items.title: OpenAI previewed GPT-5.6 for partners
items.oneliner: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 adds subagents and an ultra reasoning mode, but limited access keeps most teams benchmarking from the sidelines.

items.title: GLM-5.2 is winning security benchmarks
items.oneliner: Semgrep says GLM-5.2 beat Claude on cyber benchmarks, giving security teams another serious model option beyond US incumbents.

items.title: DeepSeek pushed speculative decoding into practice
items.oneliner: DeepSeek's DSpark claims 60 to 85 percent faster generation, making inference engineering the new model race.

items.title: AI is hitting entry-level jobs
items.oneliner: Stanford-linked tracking shows 22 to 25 year-olds in AI-exposed jobs are down 16 percent since late 2022.

items.title: Age checks are becoming speech controls
items.oneliner: Europe's chat control push and age verification laws are converging into identity-linked speech infrastructure, not child safety.

items.title: Brown caught mass AI exam fraud
items.oneliner: A Brown professor says more than 50 students used AI on one midterm, and universities still lack a response.

items.title: Cloudflare found a bug in hyper
items.oneliner: Cloudflare uncovered and patched a bug in Rust's hyper library, a reminder that core HTTP plumbing still deserves paranoia.

items.title: Export controls are reshaping model access
items.oneliner: Austria wants Anthropic hosted in Europe after US access curbs, showing geopolitics now decides who can use frontier models.

items.title: China took back the TOP500 crown
items.oneliner: China's LineShine hit 2.198 exaflops and reclaimed number one, proving CPU-heavy supercomputing is not done yet.

items.title: Memory and compute constraints are tightening
items.oneliner: HBM prices, a memory price-fixing lawsuit, and Google throttling Meta all point to AI's real bottleneck: scarce compute.
